    key_point => string (776) "Icing sugar is typically made from granulated refined sugar that comes from ...
    $value[0]['key_point']
    Icing sugar is typically made from granulated refined sugar that comes from sugarcane. The sugar is finely ground into a powder and mixed with a small amount of cornstarch to prevent clumping (as an anti-cacking agent). Addition of corn flour in a small fraction (1.7%) has not affected the character of sugar and therefore not subjected to the exclusions as specified under Note 2 to Chapter 17 on EN page IV-17-1 or the exclusions specified under Paragraph 2 of General EN to Chapter 17 on the same page. The subheading 1701.99 is further divided into National sub divisions based on the Polarimeter reading. Accordingly, cane sugar having a polarimeter reading of more than or equal to 99.7 0Z falls within the meaning of NSD 1701.99.10. Hence, NSD 1701.99.10 is ruled out.

The film is supplied in rolls exceeding 20 cm in width and does not contain any safety or anti-shatter features.

    The 5 step fan controller unit functions as both a switch and a speed regulator, using a rotating mechanism connected to six resistors and three capacitors on a PCB. Although multiple headings were considered for classification, HS heading 8414.90 (parts of an electric fan) was ruled out because the unit qualifies as a complete good under Chapter 85. Likewise, 8537.10 (panels or consoles for electricity control) was excluded due to the unit’s simplicity and lack of integrated components typically required for this heading. Based on its design and operation—especially its change over switch capability with variable resistance—the item best aligns with HS heading 8536.50 (other switches), and more specifically, 8536.50.20, which covers switches with an on off and regulatory function.

    $value[93]['key_point']
    Heading 85.31 is for electric sound or visual signaling apparatus (for example, bells, sirens, indicator panels, burglar or fire alarms). The main requirement of the product is to detect and identify any metal contaminants present in the production line. The buzzer and the LED available in the system is merely for the indication of such detection. Therefore, cannot be considered under 85.31. There is no other heading that describes the product specifically in Chapter 85, but explanatory notes to the chapter in page XVI-8543-2 mentions that the heading includes: (3) Mine detectors based on the change of magnetic flux produced in the apparatus when brought near to a metal object. Similar detectors are used, for example, for detecting foreign metallic bodies in casks of tobacco, food products, timber, etc., and for locating buried pipes. Therefore, the product merits being classified under 85.43.

    The product is a formulated mixture of two isothiazolinone biocides (5-Chloro-2-methyl-4-isothiazolin-3-one and 2-Methyl-4-isothiazolin-3-one) stabilized with magnesium nitrate, and thus does not qualify as a “separate chemically defined organic compound” under HS 2942.     key_point => string (953) "The EN to heading 92.09 on page XVIII-9209-3 paragraph (l) specifies that th...
    $value[128]['key_point']
    The EN to heading 92.09 on page XVIII-9209-3 paragraph (l)  specifies that the heading could include ""Stands"" provided that they are designed for holding an instrument. i.e. ""Stands (other than monopods, bipods, tripods and similar articles, of heading 96.20) for holding an instrument (e.g. side-drums or saxophones)"". As such, certain musical steel stands are covered by HS heading 9202.99 as music holders for fixing to instruments. Certain stands such as music-stands or desks constructed for placing on the floor or ground have been directed to HS heading 94.03. While heading 96.20 covers monopods, bipods, tripods and similar articles, the key characteristics of the products that qualify within the heading 96.20 is that they are capable of reducing the random movement of the instrument/equipment fixed onto it.  On this basis, provided musical stands do not come within the meaning of the heading 96.20, hence HS heading 96.20 is excluded.

    key_point => UTF-8 string (1097) "The drying room is constructed using insulated metal panels which are design...
    $value[173]['key_point']
    The drying room is constructed using insulated metal panels which are designed with groves. A set of fans is used to circulate hot air throughout the drying room. Trolleys with wheels are used to facilitate the movement of goods inside the chamber ensuring an equal drying of goods. Goods imported and presented separately belong to a complete dryer machine of which the heat source is a heat pump. Explanatory notes page XVI-7 further clarifies, “Note 3 to Section XVI need not be invoked when the composite machine is covered as such by a particular heading, for example, some types of air conditioning machines (heading 84.15)”. If the heat pump had been imported and presented separately, it would have been classified within 84.18 as a heat pump. As per the specification  on the catalogue, six number of trolleys are utilized  in the drying chamber. Therefore the trollies become an integral part of the drying machine and can be classified with the dryer. The drying machine has many applications and hence with the application of GIR 3 (C) 8419.39 qualifies as the appropriate HS code.

    $value[223]['key_point']
    As per EN on page XVI-8418-2 the compressor, the condenser and the evaporator are considered as essential elements for a product to be classified as a freezer or refrigerator. 
GIR 2(a) directs that products presented unassembled or disassembled are to be classified in the same heading as the assembled article. However, EN to GIR (VII) on page GIR-3 explains that no account is to be taken in that regard of the complexity of the assembly method. However, the components shall not be subjected to any further working operation for completion into the finished state. The manufacturing process provided by the importer confirms that the assembly includes further working and machine operations and that the local value addition exceeds 40% of the final product price. Therefore, the unassembled parts of the freezer cannot be considered as an assembled freezer by application of GIR 2(a). The separate parts to be classified separately according to their constituent materials and functions.

    key_point => string (1168) "A liquid Embolic System is used for Embolization. A health care professional...
    $value[255]['key_point']
    A liquid Embolic System is used for Embolization. A health care professional will inject a precise amount of the contents of the liquid embolic system into a blood vessel. Each embolic agent, which can compress substantially, will flow through the blood stream until it reaches a vessel that is too small for it to pass through. It will therefore, like a naturally formed embolism, block the flow of blood through that vessel. 
Any item that does not achieve its intended purpose in or on the body by chemical action or by being metabolized can not be a medicament and as such this item is not classified under HS Heading 30.04.
Other artificial parts of the body describes in note III (C) to the Explanatory Note in HS heading 90.21 page XVIII-9021-4 does state that they "Wholly or partially replace defective parts of the body.'" This artificial embolism is installed to achieve a specific medical purpose. This is a part that replaces defective parts such as stents, which replace damaged blood vessels. A stent opens the blocked blood vessels and this embolic agent blocks the blood vessel that should be blocked in permanently for necessary medical purposes.

    The product is a spool of plastic strip which is not put up on a support. It is a flat strip of polypropylene with an average apparent width of 1.5mm and a decitex value of 6,840dtex whilst the weight of the spool is 533g. 
Accoding to the EN to the heading 39.23, it covers all articles of plastics commonly used for packing or conveyance of all kinds of products and the product under consideration does not fit the description. Hense, 39.23 may be ruled out. 
Heading 39.20 excludes cellular products and strips of plastics of an apparent width not exceeding 5mm and hence ruled out.
Table I to section XI on page XI-12 of the EN describes decitex value of products and since it is less than 10,000 heading 56.07 is ruled out.
As the product consists a strip of a synthetic textile material with an apparent width not exceeding 5mm, it can be classified under 54.04.
